channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: New Jersey Sues Discord Over Alleged Safety Misleading and Online Predator Concerns
item.description: New Jersey Attorney General Matthew Platkin has filed a lawsuit against Discord, alleging the popular messaging app misled parents about its safety features.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: No Nvidia? No problem - Huawei debuts AI system that's apparently faster than the market leader, the GB200 NVL72
item.description: Huawei debuts an AI system in China that's significantly faster than Nvidia’s market leader, the GB200 NVL72.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Caltrans using remote-controlled equipment to help clear Highway 1 slide near Big Sur
item.description: Regent’s Slide — post mile 27.8 — occurred March 9, 2024, and started seeing top-down removal of slide material by crews on April 30, 2024. The slide originated 450 feet above the roadway displacing material that engulfed Highway 1 and continuing down to the beach and ocean below.
item.dc:creator: James Herrera, The Mercury News

item.title: The US has a single rare earths mine. Chinese export limits are energizing a push for more
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — America’s only rare earths mine heard from anxious companies soon after China responded to President Donald Trump’s tariffs this month by limiting exports of those minerals used for military applications and in many high-tech devices.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: 8 ways diversity and inclusion help teams perform better
item.description: Diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI) initiatives have been taking a beating lately. On his first day in office, US President Donald Trump issued an executive order calling government efforts to advance racial equity and to support underserved communities “illegal and immoral discrimination.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: How to deal with tech tariff terror
item.description: Are you confused about what President Donald J. Trump is doing with tariffs? Join the crowd; we all are. But if you’re in charge of buying PCs for your company (because Windows 10 officially reaches end-of-life status on Oct. 14) all this confusion is quickly turning into worry.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Star Wars Celebration is in full swing, and Lucasfilm just dropped more details on its Beyond Victory experience for Meta Quest, and I couldn't be more stoked
item.description: If you're in Japan for Celebration, be sure to play the Beyond Victory experience for Meta Quest demo.
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: Google just lost a major ad tech antitrust case. What happens next could rewire the web
item.description: Google has acted illegally to maintain a dominant position in online advertising, a federal judge ruled on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Google ruled an online ad monopoly, could be forced to break up its advertising products
item.description: In a landmark decision, a US District Judge has ruled that Google illegally monopolized the ad tech market.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google's digital ad network declared an illegal monopoly, joining its search engine in penalty box
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google has been branded an abusive monopolist by a federal judge for the second time in less than a year, this time for illegally exploiting some of its online marketing technology to boost the profits fueling an internet empire currently worth $1.8 trillion.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Netflix's first quarter builds on recent momentum as trade war drags down other tech companies
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Netflix fared better than analysts anticipated during the first three months of the year, signaling the world’s largest video streaming service is still thriving as President Donald Trump’s policies cast a pall on the economy.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: He built an AI app to beat coding interviews. Then Columbia suspended him
item.description: A software application called Interview Coder promises to help software developers succeed at technical job interviews—by surreptitiously feeding them answers to programming questions via AI.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: Sea Turtle Populations Show Signs of Recovery in Many Places
item.description: A new global survey reveals that endangered sea turtles are showing signs of recovery in a majority of places where they're found worldwide.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Enter the parallel universe of Java’s Vector API
item.description: If there is one thing you can describe as an obsession for both developers and devops, it’s how to improve the performance of applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Tesla’s first quarter EV registrations slump 15.1% in California
item.description: Tesla‘s electric-vehicle registrations in California dropped 15.1% during the first quarter, industry data showed, signaling an accelerated decline and growing challenges for the Elon Musk-led automaker in its biggest U.S. market.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Proposed rule change on endangered species triggers alarm for environmentalists
item.description: The Trump administration plans to rewrite part of the Endangered Species Act that prohibits harming the habitats of endangered and threatened species.
item.dc:creator: Tammy Webber, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg wraps up testimony in antitrust case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg emphasized in federal court on Wednesday that he bought Instagram and WhatsApp because he saw value in the companies — not to take out competitors, as the Federal Trade Commission alleges in a historic antitrust trial.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: EU equips US-bound staff with burner phones
item.description: The European Commission has issued so-called burner phones and simple laptops to certain staffers traveling to the US to reduce the risk of espionage, according to The Financial Times.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Intel sells off majority stake in its FPGA business
item.description: Intel has spun off its programmable solutions group as a standalone FPGA company, selling a majority stake in the company to a private equity firm. Intel is taking a fairly hefty loss on this deal.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Trump, Musk, RFK Jr., Lutnick make Time's 'most influential' list
item.description: Time magazine has named President Trump and five members of his administration to its list of the 100 Most Influential People of 2025, highlighting their significant impact on immigration, foreign policy, international trade, and the federal workforce.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg takes the stand in historic antitrust trial
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg took to the witness stand on the first day of a historic antitrust trial to defend his company against allegations it illegally monopolized the social media market.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Accelerate Together
item.description: Use APIs to understand cloud vulnerabilities, recommends the public sector solutions engineering lead for Wiz. The post Accelerate Together — Zero Trust 2025: Wiz’s Chris Saunders on extending ZTA to cloud first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Intel sells majority stake in Altera to Silver Lake investors in a deal valued at $8.75 billion
item.description: Intel announced Monday that it was selling the majority of its Altera chip business to technology investor Silver Lake in a deal valued at $8.75 billion.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As legacy of Ford fades in Brazil, China's BYD looks to extend global lead in EV sales
item.description: CAMACARI, Brazil (AP) — When Chinese automaker BYD Co. announced plans to build a massive factory in the hardscrabble city of Camacari, in Brazil’s northeastern Bahia state, locals saw a new beginning. After years of economic stagnation following Ford Motor Co.
item.dc:creator: FABIANO MAISONNAVE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Sony hikes PlayStation 5 console prices for Europe, UK and Australia citing economic turmoil
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Sony said it will raise prices starting Monday for some PlayStation 5 video game consoles in Europe, Australia and New Zealand, citing global economic turmoil.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Via porn, gore and ultra-violence, extremist groups are sinking hooks online into the very young
item.description: PARIS (AP) — After his arrest, the boy's mother was stunned to discover that her 12-year-old had been learning how to kill and gorging on videos of decapitation and torture so gruesome they made even case-hardened French court officials look away.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Japanese leader says he hopes Osaka expo will help reunite a divided world
item.description: OSAKA, Japan (AP) — Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba, speaking at Saturday's opening ceremony for the Osaka expo, expressed his hope that the event will help restore global unity in a world plagued by conflicts and trade wars.
item.dc:creator: AYAKA McGILL and MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KRON4
